title: The Bear Who Loved Honey Dreams
content:
In a cozy den beneath the roots of an old oak tree, a young bear named Bruno lay curled up in a ball. It was the first of November, and all the other forest bears had already fallen asleep for their long winter rest. But Bruno's dark eyes were wide open.

"I don't want to sleep!" said Bruno, sitting up and bumping his head on the low ceiling. "If I sleep, I will miss everything! What if the other animals play without me? What if something wonderful happens?"

Mama Bear came and sat beside him. She smoothed the soft fur on his head with her big, gentle paw. "My dear little Bruno," she said, "I know you are afraid of missing things. But let me tell you the secret of winter sleep."

"What secret?" asked Bruno, suddenly curious. Mama Bear smiled warmly. "When bears sleep through winter, we dream wonderful dreams. We dream of sunny days by the river and golden honeycomb dripping with sweet, sweet honey."

Bruno's eyes went wide. "Real honey?" he whispered. "The most delicious honey you can possibly imagine," said Mama softly. "Every drop as sweet as the best summer day. And the longer you sleep, the sweeter the honey dreams become."

Bruno lay down slowly, pulling his soft paw over his nose. "Wake me when the honey dreams start," he murmured drowsily. Mama Bear chuckled and tucked him in snugly. Outside, the first snowflakes were falling. Bruno breathed in and out, slower and slower, until a small smile spread across his sleeping face. He was dreaming of golden meadows and honeybees, and all was well.
